title: KeyId::ERASED sentinel plus Drop leak warning is the right RAII shape for HSM cache slots
description: key.rs, aes.rs, ecc.rs et al: each key type stores a KeyId that starts non-zero. evict() calls mem::replace(&mut self.id, KeyId::ERASED) before calling key_evict so that the subsequent Drop check sees ERASED and stays silent. If a key is dropped without eviction, Drop emits an eprintln warning with the leaked ID. A future contributor might suggest making Drop call key_evict automatically. That is impossible: key_evict needs &mut Client which is unavailable in Drop. The with_key! macro is the composable RAII abstraction; manual evict() is the explicit fallback. The leak warning fires at exactly the moment the slot is lost, with the ID, without panicking in Drop (which is unsound).
